Empires & Puzzles Jove Review: The Masked God of the Sky

Jove was once the undisputed ruler of the arena, raining down lightning and despair. But with years of power creep, has this Masked God lost his spark? We dive into his stats, skills, and whether he still deserves a spot on your team.
Jove Review: Is the Masked God Still Worthy?
Jove, The Masked God of the Sky: From Overpowered Icon to Glass Cannon

Jove. The name alone used to send shivers down the spines of diamond-tier raiders. Released in late 2022 as part of the "Carnival of Gods" (Masquerade) event, this Holy Ranger was the definition of power creep—until he became the victim of it. Once the undisputed king of damage, he now sits in a strange limbo: his special skill is still mechanically broken, but his stats belong to a bygone era. Think of him as a retired heavyweight champion who still has a mean right hook but maybe shouldn't take too many punches.

Jove's design is imposing, featuring a golden-bearded deity sitting on a throne that looks suspiciously like he borrowed it from Odin. He’s decked out in white robes and heavy gold armor, with a mask that screams "I'm mysterious but also very expensive." It’s a classic "God of Thunder" vibe, but with a masquerade twist. He looks like he’s about to judge your puzzle-matching skills and find them wanting.

Let’s be brutally honest here. Jove’s base stats (842 Attack / 793 Defense / 1406 HP) are significantly outdated. Modern legendary heroes are pushing 1300+ Attack and 2400+ HP. Jove is walking into a modern battlefield with a health pool that some modern 4-star heroes would laugh at. He is undeniably "squishy" by today's standards. However, his base attack is still respectable enough that, when multiplied by his insane special skill percentage, he can still hurt—if he survives long enough to fire.
Special Skill: Strike From the Skies
This special skill is a masterclass in utility and violence.

530% to the target is massive. Even with older stats, that multiplier hits hard. The "minor damage to all other enemies" was the result of a famous pre-release nerf (it was originally much stronger), but it still softens up the enemy team nicely.
This is where Jove still shines. A -65% healing reduction is crippling for defensive healers. But the real star is the
. This specifically counters heroes like Xnolphod, Ludwig, or any mana-boosting support. It turns an enemy's mana battery into a AA battery that’s out of juice.
Fast (8 tiles). With the Masquerade family bonus (start with minion/mana), he can effectively charge in 6-7 tiles with the right troops and team setup.

530% x 842 Attack = ~4462 raw damage output (before defense calculation). For context, a modern hero with 1300 Attack dealing 400% does ~5200. Jove lags behind, but not by as much as you'd think purely on the single-target hit because the multiplier is so high.
The -65% healing is essentially a death sentence for teams relying on slow healers to recover.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Masquerade family was ahead of its time, and parts of it have aged better than others.

Starting the battle with a Mask Minion is excellent for immediate survivability, effectively giving him a small HP buffer before the first tile is moved. The attack boost (for 2+ members) is nice, but given the rarity of these heroes, you're likely running him solo. The 8% HP minion is paper-thin against modern damage, but it's better than nothing.
This is a sneaky good passive. Since his minion is weak, it dies easily. When it does, Jove gets a free cleanse. In a meta full of DoT and defense down, this often saves him without needing a healer.

At the start of battle, damage from Special Skills is reduced. This helps him survive the first volley of fast snipers, which is critical given his lower defense stats. It gives him a fighting chance to fire at least once.
Upon release, Jove was a terror. He was the definition of "Overpowered," leading to widespread calls for nerfs. He dominated defenses and attacks alike.

Today, Jove is a classic "Glass Cannon." His damage mechanics and utility are still top-tier (Healing Cut + Mana Cut is a rare combo), but his stat line has been power-crept into oblivion. He dies to a stiff breeze compared to the 2500 HP behemoths of 2026.
. He is no longer a top-tier defense hero because he's too easy to kill. However, on
, if you can protect him (Taunt, Dodge) or fire him quickly, he can still dismantle teams. He remains a titan in events where mana control is key.
Current review focuses on the base hero as no costume information was provided or analyzed for this context.
Jove has one job: Kill things before he dies. Don't try to patch his defense; lean into his strengths.
Maximizes that juicy 530% sniper hit and the splash damage. Ensures that when he hits, he leaves a mark.
He remains fragile. If he gets hit, he folds.
Might survive one extra slash attack.
Sacrifices his only real asset (damage) for survivability that will still be subpar compared to modern heroes. Not recommended.
. You bring Jove to end the fight quickly, not to endure a marathon.
530% is elite, even if the base stats aren't.
Reducing mana from special skills (-75%) completely shuts down mana-generating tanks and supports.
-65% healing makes it nearly impossible for enemies to recover from his hit.
Passive cleansing on minion death keeps him active.
Low HP and Defense make him a primary target.
The family minion is easily swept away by modern AoE damage (though this triggers his cleanse).
The "minor damage" to others is negligible against modern defense stats.
Still great for dismantling stall teams or mana-heavy defenses, provided you bring protection.
His fast speed and debuffs are excellent here.
His mana reduction is incredible against bosses who rely on special skills.
Jove needs bodyguards. He is the VIP who does the damage, but he cannot take a punch.
Ludwig (Tank) + Jove (Flank). Ludwig forces enemies to hit him, while charging Jove. Jove fires repeatedly, preventing the enemy from gaining mana to counter Ludwig.
Jove + Anne + Constance/Reliable Healer. Fast holy damage that overwhelms the enemy before they can set up.
Fast Snipers (who can one-shot him), Minion destroyers (who remove his buffer instantly), and Taunt heroes (who absorb his single-target nuke).
Don't pair him with too many other squishy heroes. You need beefy teammates to absorb damage while Jove does the work.

If you pull Jove, level him immediately. Even with "outdated" stats, for a new player, he is a nuclear weapon. His utility will carry you through map stages and raids well into the mid-game. He is a fantastic first 5-star to max.
You likely have him benched. Is he worth a Limit Break 2 (Alpha Aethers)?

, unless you lack modern Holy hitters. The stats are just too far behind. However, he is still a valuable depth piece for War teams, especially against mana-heavy defenses where his specific debuff is needed.
The community has a love-hate relationship with Jove.
"Players: You can’t keep releasing such insanely OP heroes! SG: Hold our beers…" - This sentiment from his release sums it up. He was the poster child for "pay to win" power creep in 2022.
Many players were relieved when he was nerfed from "Minor damage to all" in beta to the live version, but he was still considered "Strongest hero in game by far" by many upon release.
Now, the sentiment is more nostalgic. "Remember when Jove broke the game?" is a common refrain as players look at their new 3000 HP monsters.

Jove is like a classic muscle car. He doesn't have the safety features (stats) of the modern models, and he guzzles gas (emblems/aethers), but when you step on the pedal, he still roars. He’s no longer the king of the gods, but he’s certainly still nobility. Use him wisely, protect him, and watch him strike from the skies one last time.
Still a viable offensive weapon, but handle with care.
